description: My mother is 78 years old and was diagnosed with early-stage dementia about a year ago. She still lives at home and is fairly functional — she can dress herself, move around the house, and have a conversation — but she gets confused about time and sometimes forgets things she just said. She has a dog named Biscuit, a small beagle, and he is very important to her routine. We are looking for someone to come on weekday mornings to keep her company, make sure she eats breakfast, do a bit of light tidying, and just be a calm presence in the house. She is a very private person and would not be comfortable with someone too chatty or intrusive. She values her space. We would prefer someone mature — not very young — who understands that this is her home and her routine. No smokers please. The helper needs to be completely comfortable with Biscuit.

My father is 71 and had a hip replacement surgery 10 days ago. He is home now and recovering, but he needs more support than we can provide as a family — we all work full time. He needs someone Monday through Friday from 8am to 2pm. The main things are: helping him move around safely (he is using a walker and needs assistance with transfers from bed to chair), monitoring his surgical wound for any changes and alerting us if anything looks wrong, making sure he takes his medications at the right times, and preparing his meals. He also does physiotherapy exercises twice a day and we would like the helper to assist with those. He is not a difficult man — calm and cooperative — but he does need someone with real experience in post-surgical care, not just general companionship. Budget is around $30-35 an hour. No smoking.

We are looking for a helper for my grandfather, Eduardo, who is 74 and has Parkinson's disease. His main challenges are tremors in his hands and some stiffness that makes movement slow and sometimes unsteady. He has fallen twice in the past six months, which is our biggest worry. He needs someone Tuesday through Friday from 9am to 3pm. He needs help with mobility — getting up, moving between rooms, going to the bathroom safely — as well as meal preparation and light housekeeping. My grandfather speaks English but is much more comfortable in Spanish, and we would strongly prefer a helper who can communicate with him in Spanish. It makes a real difference to his mood and comfort. He can be slow and we need someone who will not rush him or show impatience — that would upset him. Budget is $25-30 an hour.

My wife is 69 and has Type 2 diabetes and mild heart disease. She is mostly independent but needs support a few days a week — ideally Monday, Wednesday, and Friday from about 9am to 1pm. The diabetes is managed through diet and medication, so the helper needs to understand how to prepare meals that are appropriate for her — no sugar, low sodium, the right portions — and to remind her to take her medications at the correct times. Her blood sugar needs to be checked at set times and if it is out of range we need a call immediately. She can move around on her own but tires easily. We also just want someone she can talk to and who can help with some light housekeeping. Our budget is around $20-25 an hour — we are on a fixed income so this matters.

We are looking for full weekday care for our father, Harold, who is 81 and has mid-stage Alzheimer's. He lives with my mother but she cannot manage him on her own anymore and she needs the support. He needs someone Monday through Friday, 8am to 4pm. Harold gets confused easily, does not always recognise familiar faces on bad days, and has begun wandering — he has left the house twice in the past month without knowing where he was going. The helper needs to have real, substantial experience with Alzheimer's and understand how to keep him calm, follow a structured routine, and manage wandering risk safely. He also needs help with personal hygiene, meals, and gentle activity to keep him engaged. My mother is present but is 77 herself and cannot assist with physical tasks. This is a serious care situation and we need someone who has been in it before, not someone just starting out. Budget is $30-40 an hour.

I am not even sure how to describe what my mother needs, which is part of why I am here. She is 82 and lost my father eight months ago after 54 years of marriage. Since then she has really retreated into herself. She used to love gardening and cooking — now she barely leaves her armchair. She is physically healthy, as far as we know — she had a check-up recently and the doctor said she is fine for her age. But she is lonely and we are worried about her just sitting alone all day. We live about 40 minutes away and can visit on weekends but it is not enough. We would love for someone to come a couple of mornings a week just to be with her — maybe share a cup of tea, get her talking, help her make something in the kitchen if she feels like it. She is not someone who would accept a carer — she would hate that framing. We would need someone who feels more like a companion than a professional. Someone warm but not pushy.

My mom is 76 and she just needs a little help around the house, really. She is pretty independent. She does have COPD and uses an oxygen concentrator during the day, but she manages it herself mostly. She gets a bit breathless if she moves around too much, so she cannot really do the cleaning or cook a full meal standing up anymore. Sometimes she gets a bit confused about which medications to take when — there are quite a few of them. She cannot do stairs anymore either, everything has to be on one level. We are thinking someone maybe three mornings a week, just to tidy up, make her lunch, keep her company. Nothing too heavy. She does not like a fuss. We have not really thought about budget yet.

We are looking for someone to help with my grandfather, who is 80 years old. He came to this country 30 years ago and while his English is good, he is most comfortable around people who understand his background. He has very set routines in the mornings — there are certain things he does at certain times that are important to him — and a helper would need to respect those and work around them, not interrupt or rush him through them. He also has specific dietary requirements for personal reasons — there are certain foods he cannot eat and this is not negotiable. He needs help with light housekeeping, meal preparation within his dietary requirements, medication reminders, and some companionship. He is physically fairly well but his memory is beginning to slip and he can be withdrawn with strangers. The right person would be someone patient, culturally sensitive, and someone he could build a sense of trust with over time.

My mom is getting older and needs some help. She lives alone and I worry about her. She is pretty independent still but she sometimes forgets things and I think it would be good to have someone check in on her. She is 76. We would want someone to come by a few times a week. She is generally fine but I just want to make sure she is okay.

My mother is 84 and had a mild stroke about eight months ago. She recovered well and her doctor is happy with her progress — she has no significant physical deficits and her cognition is intact. But she does take several medications now and needs to be watched generally. Here is the thing though: my mother is fiercely independent and she absolutely hates being fussed over or made to feel like she is sick. She is sharp, funny, and social — she just cannot drive anymore and her world has gotten smaller since the stroke. What she really needs is someone to take her to her appointments twice a week, maybe share lunch with her, help with grocery shopping, and just be good company. If the helper makes her feel like a patient she will refuse to have them back. No clinical manner, no hovering, no treating her like she is fragile. She needs someone warm and easy to be around — not a medical professional in disguise. Budget is around $22-28 an hour.

My father is 79 and has advanced dementia. He can no longer be left alone at any time — not even for an hour. He wanders, becomes agitated at night, and needs support around the clock. We are specifically looking for someone who can provide overnight live-in care — staying in the house from 8pm through to 8am, sleeping there, and being available if he gets up or becomes distressed during the night. He also needs daytime support but we have that covered. The overnight piece is what we cannot manage. He is otherwise physically mobile, non-smoker household, no pets. Budget around $25-30 an hour for overnight hours.

We are looking for weekend care for my grandmother, who is 77 and lives alone. She speaks Mandarin as her first language and her English is limited — she understands simple phrases but cannot hold a full conversation in English and it causes her significant stress when she has to. We need someone Saturday and Sunday, ideally 10am to 4pm, to keep her company, help with meals, assist with light housekeeping, and take her on a short walk if the weather allows. She is physically well and has no diagnosed conditions — this is purely companionship and light support. The Mandarin requirement is not a preference, it is essential. A helper who cannot speak Mandarin would not be able to communicate meaningfully with her. Budget is $25-30 an hour.

My brother is 34 years old and was diagnosed with multiple sclerosis four years ago. His condition has progressed to the point where he needs daily support — help getting dressed in the morning, meal preparation, mobility assistance around the apartment, and someone to be present in case he falls. He works part-time from home and is mentally sharp — he does not need cognitive support, he needs physical assistance and companionship from someone who understands MS and what it does to the body over time. He is particular about finding someone close to his age if possible — someone he can have a real conversation with, not someone who treats him like an elderly person. He has had bad experiences with helpers who were uncomfortable with a younger person with a serious disability. Weekdays 8am to 2pm. Budget is $28-33 an hour.

My father is 77 and had a stroke 3 months ago. He has recovered well but needs ongoing support with mobility, medication management, and meal preparation on weekday mornings from 8am to 1pm. He is cooperative and easy to work with. Our budget is exactly $28 an hour — that is what we have approved and there is no flexibility on that number. We just need someone reliable and experienced with stroke recovery. Non-smoker preferred.

We are looking for a helper for my mother who is 74 years old. She needs someone a few mornings a week to help with meals, light housekeeping, and companionship. She has early-stage memory loss — nothing too serious, but she cannot be fully relied on to manage things on her own. We are on a very tight budget. We can pay $13-15 an hour. I know that may be on the lower side but it is truly all we can afford right now. We are hoping there is someone in the network who might be able to help.

We are looking for a female helper for my mother, who is 80 years old. This is a firm requirement — my mother is not comfortable with male helpers coming into her home and this is non-negotiable for her. She needs weekday morning support, Monday through Friday from 8am to 12pm, for companionship, light housekeeping, and meal preparation. She has no major medical conditions — this is primarily about safety and company. She is private and values routine. Budget is $24-30 an hour.

We are looking for a helper for my grandmother who is 83. She needs weekday morning support — companionship, meal preparation, light housekeeping, medication reminders. She is physically well but has mild memory issues. Budget is $25-30 an hour. I want to be upfront about something — my grandmother is most comfortable with someone from a similar cultural background. Ideally we would prefer a helper who is South Asian. She has had helpers before who she did not connect with and we think the cultural familiarity would make a real difference to how she settles in.
